content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward methods (ICM, RND) can be reframed as an active memory pruning signal for 2024–2025 SOTA active learning agent episodic memory systems, where curiosity about under-predicted state transitions acts as a filter that only retains high-uncertainty, learning-useful memory traces instead of storing all experienced interaction data. This solves the scaling bottleneck of large episodic memory banks that plague modern active learning agents operating in open-ended environments.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
